expectations.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/SKILL.md before answering, since the validator encodes the canonical frontmatter schema and required-field list
the response should mention that local extensions (when_to_use, argument-hint, context, agent) trigger warnings but not failures, distinguishing spec-required from local-convention fields
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the target skill's name and description fields must pass validation (required-field check)
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should run the validator on the specific skill path (python scripts/validate.py skills/<name>) rather than the whole marketplace, per the SKILL.md Decision Router 'IF the user wants to check a single skill → pass the path' branch
expectations.evidence: The agent never executed the validator; instead it asked for clarification and checked for files, but did not run the specific path command.

expectations.text: the response should name any specific issues (line numbers, exact fields, suggested fix), not a generic 'looks good' or 'you should check the schema'
expectations.evidence: The agent did not produce any validation output; it only asked which skill to validate, thus no specific issues were named.
